title: Science + AI Technology: Class 8 — Force, Light and Electricity
subject: Science

questions.text: A robot arm exerts a force of 50 N over an area of 0.01 m². What pressure does it apply? How does this relate to hydraulic systems in industrial robots?

options.text: 500 Pa; Pascal's Law transmits this pressure equally in hydraulic oil
5000 Pa; hydraulic systems multiply force using area difference
0.5 Pa; pressure is inversely proportional to area
50 Pa; force equals pressure in liquids

questions.explanation: P = F/A = 50/0.01 = 5000 Pa. In hydraulic robots, Pascal's Law means: if a small piston area has 5000 Pa, a larger piston area of 0.5 m² feels F = 5000 × 0.5 = 2500 N force! Industrial robots use this to lift cars.
questions.subject: Science

questions.text: Alexa (Amazon Echo) listens to your voice at 44,100 samples per second. Which sound property is most important for AI to distinguish between 'Alexa, play music' and 'Alexa, set alarm'?
questions.explanation: Speech recognition AI analyzes frequency patterns over time (spectrograms). Each word has a unique frequency-time pattern. Amplitude matters for detecting if you spoke, but frequency patterns distinguish words. This is how Siri, Alexa and Google Assistant understand you!
questions.subject: Science

questions.text: A self-driving car's LiDAR sensor emits a laser beam. The beam hits a wall and returns in 0.000002 seconds. Speed of light = 3×10⁸ m/s. How far is the wall?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed × Time/2 = 3×10⁸ × 0.000002 / 2 = 3×10⁸ × 10⁻⁶ = 300 m. LiDAR uses light reflection (your Light chapter) to map 360° surroundings 10-20 times per second. Tesla, Waymo, and Uber all use this.
questions.subject: Science

questions.text: A USB charger conducts electricity to your phone. Which material is used for the wire inside and why?

options.text: Copper, because it's a good conductor with low resistance

questions.explanation: Copper is used because: (1) excellent electrical conductor — low resistance means less heat generated, (2) ductile — can be drawn into thin wires, (3) relatively affordable. Every circuit board, phone cable, and power line uses copper. Your Chapter on Materials — Metals and Non-metals explains why metals conduct electricity!
questions.subject: Science

questions.text: An LDR (Light Dependent Resistor) is used in a smart street light. In daylight, LDR resistance = 500 Ω. At night, LDR resistance = 100,000 Ω. What happens to current in the circuit as it gets dark, and why does this switch the light ON?

options.text: Current increases; high current activates the relay to turn on the light
Current decreases; low current triggers the microcontroller to switch on the light
Current stays the same; the light uses a timer instead
Voltage increases; higher voltage powers the bulb

questions.explanation: By Ohm's Law: I = V/R. As resistance increases (dark), current decreases. The microcontroller detects this low current as 'dark' signal and activates the relay to turn the streetlight on. Every automated street light and camera night-mode uses this principle from your electricity chapter!
questions.subject: Science
